sourceText: To read a good book is to converse with the greatest minds.
Reading allows one to engage with the wisdom of great thinkers from the past.
The beginning of all wisdom is the conquest of fear.
True understanding and knowledge can only be achieved by overcoming fear.
Might without right is wicked; and right without might is feeble.
Emphasizes that principles of justice require the power to be enacted, and power must be used for just ends.
Forced migration due to Mongol invasions, and profound grief and despair following the sudden death of his spiritual mentor and companion, Shams-i Tabrizi.
Transmuted grief into a catalyst for spiritual growth through poetry and music, resulting in monumental works like the Masnavi that offer profound inspiration to humanity.
You are part of the thing you are seeking.
This implies that when we seek something externally, its essence is already present within us.
Don't be despondent about what you may perceive as difficulties. All of the things that happen to you, the good and the bad, are the wings upon which your soul will fly.
This suggests that even life's difficulties and painful experiences can become wings for the soul's growth.
Don't look for the light. Become the light.
